title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
summary: The video outlines a workflow to build a self-improving knowledge base for Hermes by connecting an Obsidian wiki to Claude and NotebookLM, using Karpathy's LLM wiki principles. Automatic cron jobs ingest data from Granola and other sources so the agent can reference emails, meeting notes, and research without forgetting context.
writeup: Jack Roberts walks through a workflow for building a self-improving knowledge base for Hermes by linking an Obsidian wiki to Claude and NotebookLM, applying Karpathy's LLM wiki principles. The setup uses automatic cron jobs to ingest data from Granola and other sources, letting the agent reference emails, meeting notes, and research without losing context. The takeaway is a concrete pipeline: an Obsidian wiki as the central store, scheduled ingestion to keep it current, and connected models for retrieval over accumulated notes.
